verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The U.S.–Iran conflict is now in its eleventh consecutive night of strikes, and oil pushed to a fresh five-week high — WTI ~$85, Brent above $91 and briefly topping $95 — reviving inflation fear and pressuring futures. S&P futures are down ~0.4%, Nasdaq futures ~−1% as chips wobble ahead of the first Magnificent-Seven prints. VIX sits near 17.5 and P(≥1% gap) is 34% — just under the 35% trigger, composite 51 (WARM). A live, open-ended war plus an oil shock plus GOOGL & TSLA earnings tonight (AMC) lock GREEN out: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and the mega-cap prints.

focus.jbig: Iran strikes 11th night · oil hits 5-week high · GOOGL/TSLA tonight · half size
focus.jtag: Eleventh straight night of U.S. strikes on Iran, oil at a 5-week high, futures slip ahead of first Mag-7 prints after the close
focus.jnote: Two forces still tug the tape, and overnight the oil side won. U.S. Central Command carried out its eleventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran since President Trump declared the ceasefire “over,” while Iran attacked U.S. facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan and Kuwait intercepted Iranian drones. Oil settled at a five-week high Tuesday and climbed further overnight — WTI ~$84–85, Brent above $91 and briefly over $95 — on fears of supply disruption, with Yemen’s Houthis now threatening Saudi shipping in the Bab el-Mandeb. Secretary of State Rubio said Washington remains committed to diplomacy but questioned whether Tehran is serious. Futures point lower (S&P ~−0.4%, Nasdaq ~−1%) as chips wobble and Alphabet and Tesla report after the close. This is a live, headline-driven tape with a double earnings catalyst tonight — deploy carefully, not aggressively.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~17.5, elevated; closed 17.45 Tue (+2.3%), 52-wk range 13.38–35.30, off the highs but sticky into VIX expiration today
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines; GOOGL/TSLA/PM earnings TONIGHT AMC, INTC/IBM/TXN/TMUS Thu; flash PMIs & jobless claims Thu
World risk — eleventh straight night of U.S.–Iran strikes, Gulf bases hit, Houthis threaten Saudi shipping; diplomacy questioned by Rubio
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; VIX-futures expiration today adds a pinning/whipsaw wrinkle; curve sensitive to any oil shock
Commodities — oil at a 5-week high, WTI ~$85 on Hormuz/Red Sea risk; still the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: topped 4.6% · near year peak 4.68% · oil lifts rate-hike bets
+0.5% · just above $4,000, capped by real yields

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news — crude is at a five-week high and energy keeps catching a supply-fear bid as strikes hit night eleven. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: any credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline gaps crude back $5–7 in minutes, taking your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a YELLOW day, don’t chase a war premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Chips ripped Tuesday (Nasdaq +1.3%) on strong Taiwan/Korea export data, but they’re wobbling this morning and the SMH-vs-VIX spread is near a record, signaling huge expected chip swings. A one-day bounce is not a durable catalyst, and GOOGL/TSLA report tonight, INTC/TXN Thu — mega-cap AI-capex risk drops right into any DTE. Selling premium into a stabilization attempt ahead of the season’s biggest prints is not a probability edge. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: A bounce isn’t a catalyst, and mega-cap earnings land tonight–Thu. Don’t underwrite a chip rebound into GOOGL/TSLA/INTC week.

setups.read: Jet fuel is at a five-week high with crude near $85, a direct margin headwind — so the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a diplomacy/ceasefire headline squeezes shorts violently (mediators floated a 10-day truce this week), and VIX near 17.5 makes any bear call a snap-back hazard. A bear call into a live-war, VIX-elevated tape with active peace chatter is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Diplomacy-squeeze risk into a fuel headwind = wait. Don’t chase the short until the tape settles.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this Wednesday morning that qualifies. Super Micro jumped ~16% and Pegasus moved on prints, but they’re volatile war-tape movers, not clean Snipers, and the mega-cap wave (Alphabet, Tesla, PM tonight; Intel, IBM, TXN, T-Mobile Thu) hasn’t hit yet. Check back Thursday once the big prints land.
setups.check_note: Nothing clean to validate — no qualifying Type A candidate today.

setups.read: A war-headline, oil-shock, futures-down morning is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts. Manulife tagged a 52-week high but it’s a foreign insurer outside the clean setup, and no fresh high-volume ATH breakout is holding into an Iran-strike, pre-mega-cap-earnings open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-driven chop into mega-cap prints
scenarios.sub: Futures stay soft on oil, chips whipsaw, the tape lurches on every Iran/oil headline; all eyes on GOOGL/TSLA after the close, vol elevated

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation / Hormuz-Red Sea disruption
scenarios.sub: Strikes intensify or Houthis hit Saudi shipping; oil runs toward $95–100, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Good case — Diplomacy breakthrough + strong Mag-7 prints
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire firms up and GOOGL/TSLA beat; oil gives back the spike, relief rally in stocks and chips, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran / Strait of Hormuz / Red Sea — the U.S. struck Iran for an eleventh straight night while Iran hit facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, and Yemen’s Houthis announced a blockade threatening Saudi shipping in the Bab el-Mandeb. Any wider strike sends crude and jet fuel vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

GOOGL & TSLA earnings TONIGHT (AMC) — the first two Magnificent-Seven prints; a weak AI-capex or cloud read from Alphabet, or a margin/guidance miss from Tesla, could reignite the chip selloff and gap the whole tech tape. Keep every spread clear of each name’s print.

Oil near $85 & the 10-year — WTI at a five-week high keeps inflation risk alive; the 10Y topped 4.6%, only a few basis points below this year’s 4.68% peak, and markets now price ~55% odds of a September Fed hike. A rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.
VIX-futures expiration today — adds a pinning/whipsaw wrinkle to an already headline-jumpy tape; can amplify intraday swings that trip stops.
Flash PMIs & jobless claims (Thu 7/23) plus INTC/IBM/TXN/TMUS earnings — macro and single-stock catalysts stacked on top of the war. A weak PMI or hot claims print would compound the risk.

earnings.intro: Q2 season goes mega-cap this week — Alphabet, Tesla and Philip Morris headline tonight (Wed 7/22) after the close, with Intel, IBM, Texas Instruments and T-Mobile Thursday. With the Iran conflict still live and oil at a five-week high, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Cloud growth and AI-capex guidance are the swing factors — the market watches Alphabet’s cloud backlog and monetization for the whole tech tape. First mega-cap read of the season, and it dropped ~1.5% pre-market. Whether AI monetization justifies surging spend is the key question.
rows.angle: Do not hold a GOOGL spread through tonight’s print. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Thursday.

rows.why: Focus is on automotive margins, FSD/Robotaxi progress and guidance. Tesla has a history of dropping after reports, and high-beta gap risk into a jumpy, oil-driven war tape is elevated. A soft read here would drag the whole growth complex.
rows.angle: Do not carry a TSLA spread through tonight. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next session.

rows.gap_sub: INTC options imply a big swing
rows.why: The SMH-vs-VIX spread is near a record, signaling large expected chip swings. Any AI-capex or second-half surprise from Intel or Texas Instruments could reignite the semiconductor volatility — stacking single-stock risk on the live Iran backdrop and Thursday’s PMIs.
rows.angle: Keep any tech spread to a DTE that clears the earnings date — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail plus the PMI/claims overlap.

earnings.outro: Wed 7/22: the tape trades the Iran/oil headlines and the chip wobble into tonight’s mega-cap prints (Alphabet, Tesla, PM after the close). It stays real Thursday with Intel/IBM/TXN/T-Mobile alongside flash PMIs and jobless claims. Half size, A-grade only, DTE clear of every print, until the tape settles.
